articles.title: Behold the neuron, a complicated cell with a simple mission
articles.description: For a new study, a Yale physicist who normally probes the complexity of thousands of neurons acting in concert flips his perspective to look at behaviors of individual neurons.

articles.title: Top 10 universities in Nigeria offering computer engineering and what sets each one apart
articles.description: Universities in Nigeria offering Computer Engineering are known for innovation, research, and industry training. Discover what makes each one stand out.

articles.title: “Too precise to be accidental": Tehran researcher claims Great Pyramid was a 'cosmic beacon'
articles.description: most attention-grabbing claim is that the pyramid’s latitude, often given as approximately 29.979234° N, resembles the speed of light, 299,792,458 meters per second.

articles.title: STAT+: The China question is tearing biotech apart
articles.description: China's ascent is forcing U.S. biotech companies and investors to pick a side: Is it an ally or an existential threat?

articles.title: Africa CDC chief warns of challenges in containing Ebola outbreak
articles.description: Africa CDC Director General Dr. Jean Kaseya is leading the response to a deadly Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda. Africa CDC was the first to declare the emergency on May 15. He warns the Bundibugyo strain is more violent than previous outbreaks, with at least 80 deaths and no approved vaccines or treatments. Funerals and population movement are fueling spread. As global travel rises ahead of the FIFA World Cup, Kaseya is urgently calling for stronger screening, health worker protection, rapid diagnostics, and international funding to contain the outbreak.

articles.title: New study: This is the fruit that reduces inflammation and protects your heart
articles.description: New study suggests that pomegranate, in combination with gut bacteria activity, may assist in reducing inflammation and stabilizing processes related to heart disease.

articles.title: UK biotech research company plunges into administration - in business since 2016
articles.description: The company was founded ten years ago and is known for developing sustainable materials.

articles.title: Science Discovery Center to rise in Cebu through Anjo World, DOST partnership
articles.description: A new science-themed educational attraction, the Science Discover Center, set to rise inside Anjo World Theme Park in Minglanilla, Cebu, was formally introduced through a strategic partnership launch held on May 14, 2026. The launch gathered representatives from Anjo World Theme Park, the Department of Science and Technology (DOST), and around 43 academic institutions across Cebu for the ceremonial signing of the Memorandum of Agreement and the formation of the Academic Alliance supporting the initiative. The Science and Discovery Center is described as the first educational attraction of its kind in the country that combines science learning experiences within a [...]... Keep on reading: Science Discovery Center to rise in Cebu through Anjo World, DOST partnership

articles.title: The “impossible” LED that could change everything
articles.description: Scientists at the University of Cambridge have achieved what was once considered impossible by electrically powering insulating nanoparticles to create a completely new kind of LED. Using tiny organic “molecular antennas,” the team found a way to funnel energy into materials that normally cannot conduct electricity, producing ultra pure near infrared light with remarkable efficiency.

articles.title: Found a cracked egg – is it permissible to eat or is it dangerous?
articles.description: Found a cracked egg? You don’t always have to toss it, but follow this critical rule: Learn when it is dangerous and how to store it correctly.

articles.title: Scientists just unlocked a cheaper way to make clean hydrogen fuel
articles.description: Researchers have developed a durable new catalyst that produces clean hydrogen without relying on expensive platinum metals. The breakthrough could make renewable hydrogen fuel cheaper, more efficient, and easier to scale for real-world energy use.

articles.title: Cambridge Science Park launches £3bn expansion plan
articles.description: Hub for scientific and tech innovation aims to triple in size over next 30 years

articles.title: Quantum ghost imaging works using only sunlight in stunning new experiment
articles.description: Scientists have achieved something that once sounded almost impossible: using ordinary sunlight to create quantum-linked photon pairs, a phenomenon normally dependent on precise laboratory lasers. By building a sun-tracking system that funnels sunlight through optical fiber into a special crystal, researchers generated strongly correlated photons capable of performing “ghost imaging,” where images are reconstructed indirectly through quantum correlations. Remarkably, the sunlight-powered setup produced image quality close to that of a traditional laser system, even recreating detailed images like a “ghost face.”

articles.title: Roadmap charts three paths to room-temperature quantum materials for cooler computing
articles.description: Imagine a laptop that never gets hot, a phone that holds its charge for days, or a computer memory chip designed to permanently retain data, even when the power goes out. This is the possibility sitting inside a remarkable family of materials that a team of researchers from the University of Ottawa and the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) has spent years trying to understand, and they just published a comprehensive roadmap of the field to date in the journal Newton.

articles.title: Zinc is a metal essential to life – scientists have discovered a protein that helps keep cells alive when zinc levels are low
articles.description: While iron and calcium are the metals that get the most attention, zinc is also important for human health and function.

articles.title: The Quest for Clean Hydrogen Moves Underground
articles.description: The dream of clean hydrogen has tantalized energy experts for years, but producing it has been tough. Many start-ups think the answer could lie beneath our feet.

articles.title: Oldest surviving English poem in history discovered in manuscript in Roman library
articles.description: Researchers from Ireland's Trinity College discovered a rare copy of “Caedmon’s Hymn” from the 7th century in a manuscript in Rome.

articles.title: How looking through static can help people with a common degenerative disease see better
articles.description: Age-related macular degeneration is a common cause of blindness, but adding some static noise to a patient’s vision can counteract some of the disease’s effects.

articles.title: eROSITA discovers a 'changing-look' Seyfert galaxy
articles.description: Astronomers have tracked a dramatic "changing-look" active galactic nucleus (AGN) whose central supermassive black hole appeared to switch off and then rapidly reignite. The galaxy, HE 1237−2252, dimmed in X-rays by a factor of 17 within just 18 months before recovering again. The paper outlining its analysis was uploaded to the arXiv preprint server on May 8.

articles.title: Are you also participating in the steps trend? Look what it does to your body
articles.description: A massive study by Clalit among approximately 600,000 users discovered that step competitions in applications lead to a significant decrease in the risk of diabetes, stroke, and heart disease.

articles.title: China showcases space film, AI innovations at Sofia Science Festival
articles.description: SOFIA, May 17 (Xinhua) -- China showcased its achievements in space exploration, artificial intelligence and scientific cooperation at this year's Sofia Science Festival, which concluded on Sunday at Sofia Tech Park after four days of exhibitions, lectures and interactive events.

articles.title: Breaking Down the Relative Strength Index Revolution in Global Equity Analysis
articles.description: The relative strength index has emerged as the cornerstone of modern global equity intelligence, fundamentally reshaping how institutional investors and portfolio managers analyze market momentum across international securities. This technical indicator, originally developed by J. Welles Wilder Jr. in 1978, has evolved into an indispensable tool for navigating the complexities of today’s interconnected global markets.... articles.description: “Our findings show that intestinal stem cells are not only responsible for tissue regeneration, but also the early defense against bacterial infection,” explained study supervisor Dr. Matan Hofree.

articles.title: Iran eyes a new source of power deep beneath the Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: Emboldened by its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, Iran is turning to one of the hidden arteries in the global economy: subsea cables beneath that carry vast internet and financial traffic between Europe, Asia and the Persian Gulf.

articles.title: 'At least four alien species' found in crashed UFOs, ex-CIA-funded researcher claims
articles.description: A former CIA-funded researcher has made an astonishing claim, saying that "at least four" different extraterrestrial species have been discovered in crashed UFO wreckage recovered by the US – though no physical evidence has been submitted to back up the claims
